title: Find the Occurrence of First Almost Equal Substring
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ performance proposals for this challenge. The minimum-footprint C virtual-Z variant is implemented in C; see crossReferences.

variants.languageReason: The workload is two linear string scans over n + m <= 200001 characters. C++ gives native char access, contiguous vector<int> storage, no GC/interpreter overhead, and simple ownership for the Z buffers.
variants.approachJustification: Run Z on pattern + delimiter + s for prefix LCPs, run it again on reversed inputs for suffix LCPs, and return the first window where left + right >= m - 1.

variants.languageReason: Extended KMP keeps the same linear bound but avoids building concatenated strings, so the hottest loops compare the original string buffers directly with compact vector<int> LCP arrays.
variants.approachJustification: Compute direct LCP arrays from s to pattern and from reversed s to reversed pattern using the pattern Z array, then scan windows once.

reasoningSummary: A window of length m is almost equal to pattern iff the longest exact prefix match plus the longest exact suffix match covers at least m - 1 positions. Prefix and suffix match lengths for every candidate window are computed in linear time with Z/Extended KMP.
